OwnerName: K T J Dixon & N J McAlister
Spotlight: Terrific in the Qld Day Stakes Eagle Farm two back. Close-up and gave his all to just miss at Ipswich. Not convinced about the mile or gate however.
Narrow miss in the Cooney at Ipswich 20 Jun. A few runs under his belt this campaign and can make his presence felt with even luck.

OwnerName: O T I Hillier, A H Collins Et Al
Spotlight: Made late rally from the back at Ipswich. Up to 1600m can help, and can sit closer from good gate. Improver.

OwnerName: Mrs E Beak, J C Beak & Mrs V G J Beak
Spotlight: On a tight rein and mowed them down late in the TL Cooney at Ipswich. Featured both times here. Can get the mile with a quiet ride.

OwnerName: Finemore Thoroughbreds, E J Lucas Et Al
Spotlight: Well back and made limited headway at Eagle Farm at this trip. Can improve on a drier deck, and in a slightly easier race.

OwnerName: Australian Bloodstock, F Meduri Et Al
Spotlight: On a winning streak. Showed plenty of fight to secure victory this track. Good draw. Sterner test here but could run a race at odds.

OwnerName: Mrs C L Munce, M P Rafter Et Al
Spotlight: Responded well from back half to get the prize at Gold Coast. Soft proven. Capable of figuring up in class with even luck.
Settled back and didn't figure in the 3yo (Open) SW at Ipswich. Has to lift considerably.

OwnerName: D R Foster, L K Wahlstrom Et Al
Spotlight: Sent out $101 when unable to threaten in the slosh at Eagle Farm at this trip. Has plenty to find on latest offerings. On trust.

OwnerName: Darby Racing, A J Barsby Et Al
Spotlight: Backing up quickly. Iron filly lining up for her 18th career start. Mile suits, and SW at this class suits. Likely improver.
Settled back and didn't figure in sloppy conditions at this trip in the Gunsynd Classic. First time here. Give her another chance.

OwnerName: P S Farrugia, D F Bonnin & F L De Pasquale
Spotlight: Raced in back half and never likely at Eagle Farm at this journey. First time around Sunshine Coast. Can't enthuse.
Refused to shirk task in choppy conditions in (Bm70) at Doomben. Others rate stronger.

OwnerName: Miss V L Thompson, Mrs J H Kemp & Ms C Kowalchuk
Spotlight: Great run at Toowoomba 13 Jun. Led, stuck on for third in the 3yo (Bm65) at Gold Coast. Tougher alley. Strikes a harder race.

OwnerName: J D Sayeg, M N Lindenmayer & B L Wheeler
Spotlight: Ran on okay without troubling at Doomben. Sterner task awaits.

Won both his trials before justifying short odds over 5f here three weeks ago when making all for a comfortable defeat of Chancheng Sparkle; 7lb worse off with the second here but that should be manageable.
Short-headed here a month ago (6f; Chancheng Sparkle in fifth) and has since filled the same berth in a trial; he might give Jedi Spurs something to think about.
Looks sure to win races but he couldn't hold a candle to Jedi Spurs over 5f here last month and will do well to reverse those placings even with a 7lb pull.

OwnerName: Alex Leung King Yue & Mok Tsz Wan
Spotlight: Beaten in trials on either side of finishing down the field here in late May when well behind Almighty Warrior.
No better than sixth in his three trials and he was another who was left for dead by Jedi Spurs here last month.
Can't beat Almighty Warrior on their C&D clash in May and beat just one home in a recent trial.

OwnerName: Li Wai Yin & Gina Li Wai Shuen
Spotlight: No worthwhile form in trials and was last here 21 days ago in the race won by Jedi Spurs.
Has won a trial and finished third in his two official races, last time a fader over C&D (just behind Almighty Warrior); has each-way claims.
Has ability but he's finished behind a few of these in his two runs here (6f/5f); vulnerable again.
Beaten in three trials, finishing third in the latest; unlikely to be good enough.
Hasn't kicked on from a promising debut here over 6f in November and was only midfield in a recent trial; this relegation into Class 5 company should at least help.
Competitive over 6f/7f in Class 4s this season and had excuses when down the field here (7f) three weeks ago; one to consider on his Class 5 debut.

OwnerName: Dr Anthony W K Chow & Michael Tong Hing Yuen
Spotlight: Another class-dropper but for good reason as he's been struggling for a while now; he didn't get the clearest of runs last time, admittedly.
Has won a Class 5 and was placed in a Class 4 on his penultimate run at Happy Valley (6f); had a bad draw there last time; possible.
Running well and he has the same draw as when making all over C&D last week; whacked up 8lb for that but still appeals more than most.
Has serious questions to answer after again failing to fire on Class 5 debut three weeks ago.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Mark Fong Chung, Matthew Fong & Victoria Fong
Spotlight: Has had near misses at Happy Valley and here so needs considering in a race of this nature from a good draw.
Unplaced in all 14 starts and his best distance remains something of a mystery; only fourth of eight in a trial late last month.
Placed off much higher mark over C&D on seasonal debut in September but subsequent form has been underwhelming; needs to turn a corner.
Only 1-22 but he finished well to press an in-form winner and Double Bingo over 7f here 13 days ago, and his only win was at 6f; considered.
Dual winner but out of form in his last three races (and subsequent trials) and wants further ideally.
Outran huge odds to be fourth over C&D last week but he's had plenty of chances now.

OwnerName: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho
Spotlight: He'll appreciate this return to dirt and the drop in grade, so having won a recent AW trial he's interesting off a good mark; well drawn as well.
0-7 and only eighth in his previous dirt run; drawn wide and struggled in a trial last time.
Both wins have come over C&D but he's been poor in his last two races and fared no better in subsequent trials.
Won narrowly over C&D in May but weakened out of it last time, again over 6f but on turf; this surface is more his scene and he's not handicapped out of it.

OwnerName: Wong Wing Keung & Cheung Ka Ho
Spotlight: All defeats have been on turf and latest effort wasn't too bad a run; if taking to this surface he might have some part to play now down in grade.

It was a Class 4 in which he was a closing sixth here two runs ago and he dropped unlucky with the draw in a Class 5 last time; failed to shine on dirt the last time he tried it but it's worth another go after winning a recent trial on the surface.
Recent form doesn't bode well but he has won on dirt and added a recent trial on the surface; not drawn well, though.
Seems better on dirt than turf and was close up behind Robot Knight over C&D in May; 4lb lower now and Zac Purton takes over.
Has only won a 5f novice and has been struggling on the grass; light on dirt experience.
Finished second in his most recent trial but on official form he can be taken on.
Consistent front-runner who was a close fourth in Robot Knight's race here in May and a recent trial run (fourth) could have sharpened him up.
Weighted to give Robot Knight plenty to think about on their clash here in May and subsequent turf run wasn't too shoddy.

OwnerName: Bryan Kwan Hon Ming, Jessica Kwan Mun Hang Et Al
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season but form has cooled of late and he beat only one home last time, albeit in a tougher Group 3 handicap; would have a chance off this mark if bringing his 'A' game.
Shock winner over 7f here in April but well beaten since and again in his latest trial; his going days are hard to predict and further suits ideally.
Generally in good form this year and was right behind Rising Force here last time despite being dropped in from his wide draw; chance.

OwnerName: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man
Spotlight: Consistent and improving 4yo who has won twice over C&D this season and hard to fault his recent efforts, even the latest when beaten only 1.5l into fifth; solid chance.
Progressive 4yo at Class 3 level in which he nearly won again here three weeks ago on dirt debut; a bit worrying that it was his first run in a Class 2 before then and he managed only fifth.
Made all to beat Rising Force over C&D in March but was behind that rival and Young Champion here a month ago, that after another modest effort.
Dual sprint winner in New Zealand but lengthy breaks are a feature in his profile and now returns from another for his new yard; third and fifth in his recent trials; likely best watched.
C&D winner in January off 7lb lower and had continued to run well until last time, since when he's had a break; possibilities.
Low mileage and has hinted at winning potential at a higher level; can lead so stall 1 comes in handy on his Class 4 debut.

Has won at trips ranging from 6f to 1m1f; after a break he took big step back in right direction when running on for fifth over 6f here in May but his following run over 7f wasn't so clever; downgraded but he's an 8yo now.

Both wins over 1m at Happy Valley in spring of 2025; had placed form through the winter - including here - and he was badly interfered with when midfield after a lengthy break three weeks ago; could go well.
6f/7f wins here were a good while back now and he'll need to perform a lot better than he has in his last two races, both over 6f at Happy Valley; returning here over 7f should at least help.

OwnerName: Dr Bosco Chan, Dr Chu Wai Ming Et Al
Spotlight: Won on soft ground in Australia but little to shout about after six starts in Hong Kong.

OwnerName: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Very opposable on what he's shown so far, though he was badly drawn when trying 7f for the first time three weeks ago.

OwnerName: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen
Spotlight: His recent improvement has come at 1m at Happy Valley, winning in May and then beaten 2.5l off this 6lb higher mark last time; however, he has won over 6f so 7f isn't an issue and Joao Moreira knows him well; not without hope despite a draw that's wider than ideal.
7-53 with the wins coming over 6f/7f; 14lb below his last winning mark but he's looked well treated for a while now.
Only had five races and the best was his second when a close fourth at Happy Valley (6f); hasn't progressed and slow starts are holding him back.
Hasn't had much racing at all and recent fourths over 6f were perfectly respectable; however, doesn't appear to be crying out for 7f and he's drawn wide.
No wins in 11 and even when placed he's not really dropped a blow on the winners; edging down the weights and his turn will probably come.
Drawn wide but still respected after recent seconds over C&D, the latest behind a consistent improver; Purton returns.
Dead-heated over 1m in Class 5 in March but it's been a struggle since; only ran last week.
Consistent in trials and on the track, on turf and dirt; did well to threaten an unexposed winner when drawn wide over C&D latest; one of the more appealing options.

OwnerName: Dr Andrew Yu, Dr Alvin Yu Man Ho & Dr Bernice Yu Man Yan
Spotlight: Five-time winner who gives his running more often than not and ran a cracker behind one better drawn on turf here a month ago; this surface is fine but stall 10 is a negative.
Dual 6f dirt winner; back on preferred surface after low-key turf run last month; however, he is 6lb above his last winning mark.

OwnerName: Dr & Mrs Edmund Hon Hing Cheung
Spotlight: Dual 6f dirt winner this year; 7lb higher than for the last of those wins and subsequent defeats suggest the handicapper may have his measure; down the field in recent trials.

13-race maiden with an up-and-down profile; he was beaten a little over 3l on his only previous run on dirt (C&D in April) but that was a Class 3 and he was close up on turf two runs ago; has a big run in him.

OwnerName: Chan Siu Cheung & Calvin Chan Wai Pong
Spotlight: Lightly raced and narrowly made a successful stable debut over C&D in January; should be sharper for finishing second in a recent trial but the draw has done him no favours.
0-5 but he's a trial winner and has been holding his own in 5f handicaps here on turf; if as effective on dirt then he shouldn't be far away; shapes as though he wants 6f.
Won a trial in March but no better than fourth in four subsequent ones; however, Zac Purton was aboard for all those defeats and he keeps the faith on official debut; drawn in 1.
It's been a quiet start in Hong Kong but he won in Australia and returns to the track after landing an AW trial; would have been half interesting but for such a wide draw.
Beaten in a bucketload of trials this year and he's probably on an unrealistic mark.

OwnerName: Alan Kiang Ping Fai & Joyce Lee
Spotlight: 1-10 on dirt and 3-19 on turf; 3lb beneath last winning mark but recent efforts not easily forgiven.
Without a win since April 2025; 1m probably too far last time and this season hasn't been without the occasional competitive show over 6f; wouldn't be a surprise should he have some say.

OwnerName: Tang Ming, James Tang Sau Yip Et Al
Spotlight: Placed twice over 5f since breakthrough win over that trip in March but form has dipped lately and he has a point to prove on this first 6f attempt.

OwnerName: Anthony Chan Wai Choi, Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung
Spotlight: Caused 69-1 surprise over 5f in first-time cheekpieces here in May, his third run, but a lesser effort followed and it's hard to know what to expect from him on this return to 6f.

OwnerName: Dr & Mrs Johnny Ng Sai Chun Et Al
Spotlight: Placed over 7f in higher-grade company here in December but has lost his form this year; needs to have been revitalised by recent stable switch.
0-9 in Hong Kong but posted a sound effort when third over C&D five weeks ago and competes off the same mark again; can make his presence felt.

OwnerName: Lo Kui Lun, Lo Hoe Et Al
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a string of good efforts over 5f/6f and is well handicapped if judged on those but has not fired on his three starts since returning from a break in April.

Placed on two of his four starts, more recently when close second from stall 10 over C&D a week ago; only slightly better drawn today but has the same mark and Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely contender.

OwnerName: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching
Spotlight: Makes official debut after a fairly encouraging trial but has a tough draw and is best watched.
Never looked dangerous when ninth of 12 on recent debut at Happy Valley; moves up from 5f today.
Soundly beaten on first three starts (6f-7f) and has wide draw today; look elsewhere.
Yet to show any significant promise after three runs (all 6f) and will probably need a drop in grade.
Six-race maiden; suffered worrying dip in form after C&D third in February but his keeping-on fourth at Happy Valley in May was much more encouraging; considered each-way.
Needs to rebound from a poor run six weeks ago but was runner-up over C&D on his two previous starts and will be a major player from stall 1 if all goes to plan.
Belied huge odds when fourth at Happy Valley (1m1f) in April, his second Hong Kong start, but well beaten twice since.

Close third over C&D when upped in trip in May and also ran well when fourth over 1m in refitted cheekpieces at Happy Valley later that month; still on a workable mark; can make his presence felt again.

OwnerName: Dr Ng Chi Ho, Dr Steve Chuang Et Al
Spotlight: Ran okay over 1m here on last two outings but remains 5lb higher than when winning narrowly over that trip at Happy Valley in February; tries this extra furlong for the first time.
Safely held here (1m) on recent stable debut and now 0-18 in Hong Kong; others have more pressing claims.
Dual 1m winner at Happy Valley this season; back on a good mark now but ran poorly when last in action and has become far too inconsistent to rely upon.
A maiden after seven starts but lost out only by a head after coming from the rear over 1m last month and promises to be suited by this extra furlong; high on the list.

OwnerName: 23/24 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Infrequent winner who has been out of form since returning from a break in May; not the most obvious answer.

OwnerName: Maurice Lai Tai Hei, John Lo Et Al
Spotlight: Led close home to open Hong Kong account over this trip on dirt last month, his 12th attempt; 6lb higher now but should be fine back on turf and further progress is not out of the question.
In good form on dirt in recent months, most notably when winning Class 5 event over today's trip four weeks ago; back up in grade today but turf also suits and he ought to run well again.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Admund Au Chi Hoe
Spotlight: Beaten only by a neck over C&D when upped in trip last month and has now been placed four times this season; drawn much wider today but seemingly close to a breakthrough win.
Yet to show anything more than minor promise after five runs (7f-1m) and probably needs a drop in grade.
Unplaced all six starts but outran triple-digit odds when fourth over C&D in May (three runs ago) and the booking of Zac Purton heightens interest.
Displayed improvement when fourth to Firefoot on last month's dirt debut (1m1f) but is quickly back up in grade today and still needs to prove himself on turf.
Runner-up over this trip at Happy Valley on three of his last five starts, albeit the latest was in lower-grade company, and has each-way claims again.
Fast-starting front-runner who won twice over C&D at start of this year and was placed on his last two outings; drawn much wider than last time, though, and might be vulnerable close home.
Gained this season's two wins over 1m on dirt but his C&D second in May was arguably his career-best performance; didn't fire on latest outing, though, and remains on a tough mark.
Placed over C&D on all four starts, the last twice when second to same exciting rival; has inside draw and the services of Zac Purton today; good chance.
Much better drawn than when eighth over C&D three weeks ago but has not yet kicked on from his winning debut here in November; others preferred.
Delivered on earlier promise when dominating from the front over C&D last month, his seventh start; has scope for further progress and can make another bold bid.
Out of a middle-distance Group 3 winner; showed only minor promise in recent trials; probably one for further down the line; blinkered on debut.
Out of a 10.5f winner; won a recent 6f dirt trial and evidently has ability; makes official debut today.
Low-mileage 3yo who displayed significant improvement when keeping-on second over C&D last month; holds each-way claims at least if able to build on that effort.
Ex-Australian 4yo; unplaced all nine Hong Kong starts and makes very limited appeal from stall 12.

OwnerName: Lau So Wah & Donna Ng Siu Ying
Spotlight: Not disgraced over 6f on first two starts (turf/dirt) but well down the field when upped to 7f six weeks ago and drawn wide for this return to sprinting.
Unable to land a telling blow on any of first four starts (5f-7f); favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
0-12 in Hong Kong and change of stables made no difference last time; usual blinkers left off today; probably needs a drop in grade.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; E200,000 breeze up buy in May; dam 5f/6f 2yo winner (inc AW; RPR 84), half-sister to winners Cupid's Glory (7f 2yo Group 3), Clinical (8.5f Group 3), Courting (1m2f Listed) and Prescription (6f Listed), out of 5f/6f winner; likely type on paper.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Mehmas filly; E135,000 yearling; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 85), half-sister to winners Smash Williams (6f 2yo Group 3), She's Trouble (6f 2yo) and Royal Entry (Listed-placed 10.4f/1m4f); from good family and respected source; can go well on debut.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Minzaal filly; E52,000 foal, E160,000 yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to North American Graded stakes-placed 5.5f 2yo/7f winner Appraise, out of 1m1f Listed winner; yard in sparkling form and rookie sire has made a decent start; check market.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Blackbeard filly was sent off the 6-4 favourite on debut over C&D last month but weakened late into fifth; likely to improve for that initial experience and McMonagle retains the ride; capable of better.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: No Nay Never filly was sent off at 7-1 on debut at the Curragh (6f, good) and weakened late into an 8.75l fifth behind well-fancied stablemate; the drop back to 5f should suit on that evidence and yard's 2yos often improve plenty second time out so not dismissed.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: No Nay Never filly; E110,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Mount Kosciuszko (Group-placed 1m-1m1f inc UAE dirt; RPR 100) and Peak Earnings (US 7.5f/1m turf); dam French 5f/6f 2yo winner (inc Group 2; 108); appealing pedigree; market can guide.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth of six on debut at Cork (5f, soft) but didn't build on that when soundly beaten over C&D (good) on her next start; stable jockey prefers the chances of My Irish Lady.

Two 2yo wins (good to firm/good to soft) including 5f nursery at Haydock on final juvenile start, and reappeared with near-miss at Wolverhampton (5f, AW); excuses on both starts since, drawn wide at Chester then helping set the race up for the closers at Sandown; not ruled out each-way.

Impressive on debut at Nottingham (5f, good) last June and respectable eighth at Royal Ascot next time; underwhelming subsequently and well beaten at 66-1 in Royal Ascot handicap (5f, good to firm) on recent reappearance; the drop in grade could trigger a revival but she has something to prove.

Made it two wins from his 11 starts when scoring at Musselburgh (5f, good) last month and respectable fifth of 13 in good 3yo handicap at York (5f, good) last Saturday; each-way claims back down in grade.

Won in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Goodwood (5f, good) in May and has continued in good heart, not beaten far when eighth of 16 in the competitive 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) before close third of four at Goodwood (5f, soft); he's in with an each-way shout.

2-2 in 2yo campaign, both wins coming last July (5f, good); then absent until 11-2 favourite and merely mid-division in competitive 3yo handicap at York (5f, good) in May; needs to leave that run well behind but she retains potential for her top yard; no surprise to see a big performance.

Prominent racer who returned to form to win over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago and he followed up at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last Saturday, with the front two clear; up 6lb for last time and this is tougher, but he's clearly progressive; key player.

Made debut in April and showed promise among his first three starts before an improved showing on handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Leicester (5f, good) last month, when he comfortably made all; 8lb higher in a better race today but he could have more left in the tank.

In good form on AW prior to respectable fourth at Sandown (5f, good) in April off 4lb higher; however, two lesser runs have followed; upset in the stalls and withdrawn at York on last intended start (22-1 at the time).

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Ringo Ng Yiu Kwok & Quentin Ng Kwan Wai
Spotlight: Three wins at Happy Valley this season, the most recent on latest outing three months ago; latest 7b rise demands more progress but he's well drawn to attack from the front again.

OwnerName: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See
Spotlight: Launched Hong Kong career with five respectable runs, finishing either fifth or six each time, but his latest 2lb drop is not enough to tempt.
Placed in 6f Listed race on last of eight starts in Australia but well down the field (albeit on dirt) on recent Hong Kong debut.

OwnerName: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al
Spotlight: Nearly caused 65-1 shock over C&D a fortnight ago and remains on a good mark but it's hard to predict whether this regressive and inconsistent 9yo will be in the right mood again.

Disappointed in first-time cheekpieces (now removed) at Happy Valley three months ago and has been deserted by Zac Purton today but was placed over C&D on first two Hong Kong starts and has obvious each-way claims if judged on those efforts.
Unexposed 3yo who won easily over C&D three weeks ago and is now 2-3; moves up in grade today but could still have a lot more to offer and commands respect.
0-7 in Australia and absent since two handicap fourths (5.5f/6f) towards end of last year; probably best watched on Hong Kong debut.
Ended three-race career in Australia with 6f maiden handicap win last November; makes Hong Kong debut after eight-month break and has double-digit draw; entitled to benefit from this outing.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Cyrus Li Kin Wai
Spotlight: Won narrowly on dirt in Class 4 in early April but has struggled on all three starts since returned to this higher grade and is drawn wide today; swaps visor for refitted blinkers.

Five-time winner, most recently from favourable draw in Class 4 company at Happy Valley in May; had tough draw when safely held on dirt last month but, nevertheless, will probably remain vulnerable in this grade.
Dual C&D winner in Class 4 company last year but safely held in this grade on last three starts and faces tough task from stall 12.
Quite lightly raced 4yo who has remained in good form since C&D win on seasonal debut in September; not beaten far when fifth from tough draw here last month and enters calculations.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Sometimes slowly away; never dangerous in Group 2 and Group 1 races on last two starts; progressive otherwise, in handicaps prior to good efforts at Listed level; could go well, back down in grade.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Useful handicapper who has shown career-best form on last two starts, winning at York (5f) then creditable third at Epsom (6f); faces a stiffer assignment on these terms, however.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Creditable second on reappearance when bidding for a second win in the Palace House at Newmarket; hindered by poor draw at Royal Ascot since; landed this contest (good to firm) 12 months ago, despite carrying 3lb extra, and has no penalty this time; major contender.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Dual Listed winner who faced a stiff task in deep waters at Royal Ascot last time; respectable third to Rumstar in this race last year and was only one place behind that rival in the 2026 Palace House; in the mix.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Australian mare who has become less consistent with age but is very smart on her day, as three Group 1 wins on British/French soil attest; showed clear signs of a return to form last time when bidding to regain her King Charles III Stakes crown; top rated, back down in grade.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly who had wind surgery prior to a heavy defeat (not beaten solely by poor draw) in the Wokingham last time, failing by a long way to record a second Royal Ascot win; best watched.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Scottish raider who has shown great progress, having gone from winning off a mark of 57 early last season to running well in Listed grade a couple of times, including on penultimate start; this is harder.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Record is 2-4; ran respectably dropped to 5f in Royal Ascot handicap most recently; may do better still but has a tall order in this field, with current rating a long way below the required standard.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: 3yo maiden who is often highly tried; seemed to run well, despite traffic issues, in the Scurry over C&D on penultimate start; however, chance of finally winning depends on the effects of new headgear.

OwnerName: Anoj Don, Daniel Macauliffe Et Al

Spotlight: C&D winner courtesy of success in valuable Ballyhane Stakes last year; ran well here over 6f when fourth in handicap on return (beaten 3l by Keke) before landing handicap back to 5f at Navan next, pulling well clear with the second; 17lb worse off with Keke now and has to put a poor effort behind him at Fairhouse 29 days ago.

Seven-time sprint winner, landed a C&D handicap off 97 in April, making all to beat Indigo Dream by 2.5l; shaped well when third in Listed company over C&D in May (Likedbymike in fourth); beaten favourite 20 days ago when fifth at Cork (Likedbymike second) but wasn't disgraced and likely to bounce back at a track he enjoys.

OwnerName: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy
Spotlight: Sole win in 18 starts came in Cork maiden in October 2024; produced career best over C&D in April when 0.75l second to subsequent Group 1 scorer Mission Central in Listed race; finished ahead of Keke when filling the same spot at Cork last month and line can be put through handicap run six days ago as gaps didn't come; leading player if not backing up too fast.

OwnerName: P J O'Brien, Mrs P Shanahan & M O'Flynn
Spotlight: Very useful two-year-old last year, second to Charles Darwin on debut before finishing fourth in Queen Mary; landed a Cork maiden in cosy fashion on return before finishing 14th of 15 in Listed company but still only beat 6l; travelled well before flattening out when seventh in competitive Ayr sprint 14 days ago on soft; open to improvement and can figure on these terms.

Improved plenty on three juvenile runs in two starts this year, finishing runner-up to the useful Namiid, pulling well clear of third, at Navan (5f, y) before landing Cork maiden on good in straightforward fashion by 1.5l to Sparky Sparky 20 days ago; bare form still a level below what's required here but should continue to progress.

Won two 1m4f handicaps, gamely making all at Chester on second occasion; pitched in at the deep end for Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm) 17 days ago, in which he was a never-dangerous eighth of 11 at 100-1, but that's earned him a 9lb rise; proving up to that task probably requires further improvement.

Completed a hat-trick by scoring at York (1m4f, good) in May; 16-1 when dropped in from a wide draw in 19-runner King George V Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) 16 days ago and ran on well from out the back to finish fifth; raised another 4lb and this extra 2f poses a question but there's no sign yet of getting to the bottom of him.

Best form when second on handicap/seasonal debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April; however, heavy defeats since in the Lingfield Oaks Trial, a Goodwood handicap and the King George V at Royal Ascot (80-1), all at about 1m4f, leave her with a lot to prove.

Second to Magnetude at Chester (1m4f, good) in May on handicap debut; 10-11 for seven-runner race at Newbury (1m4f, good to soft) on latest outing but drawing 4l clear represented improved form and gave plenty of hope for 1m6f; up 7lb but considered for yard which also runs Hard To Believe.

1m, heavy-ground winner as 2yo; emerged in second in Doncaster handicaps this season over 1m4f and an extended 1m6f, so he brings proven stamina and effectiveness on good to firm; up 3lb but more to give.

OwnerName: Jayar Investments & Mrs L E Ramsden
Spotlight: Seventh to Arc Ole Ole when 2-1 favourite on handicap debut at York in May; made favourite again, however, in six-runner race here (again 1m4f on good) 15 days ago and always close up to win by a neck, finding plenty for sustained pressure; up 3lb but it looks as if 1m6f will suit him well.

Carries his head high; 7l fourth of 14 to Arc Ole Ole at York (1m4f, good) in May on handicap debut; bettered that form when staying on best to win a four-runner race at Sandown (1m6f, good) three weeks ago, but 3lb higher today and his sights are raised.

8,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Evelyn's Phoenix (7f-1m2f; RPR 72) and Racing Ray (Slovakian/Serbian 6f/8.5f including 2yo); dam Austrian 5f-7f winner, sister to Maxi Boy (Group-placed 6f 2yo); one of two newcomers for the stable; betting to guide.

Beaten favourite in a 5f novice at Southwell on his debut in May; blinkered for his second run, showing a bit of improvement when third of the six runners in a 6f maiden at Chester last week; drops in grade but improvement is required.

Down the field on debut but he ran well when second in a 7f seller at Chester (good to soft) last month; visored (absent today) when second in a 6f novice at Chepstow last week; one of two runners for the trainer who won this race in 2022 and 2025.

Fourth foal; half-brother to winners Sourire Secret (1m; RPR 66) and Claim That Smile (7f; 64); dam 1m 2yo winner (stayed 1m6f; 66), half-sister to 1m-1m4f winner Barwick; one of two newcomers for Stuart Williams.
Modest form in two maiden runs last month; new blinkers need to have a galvanising effect.
Two good runs and two lesser ones this season, failing to improve for a sixth furlong/drop to selling company at York three weeks ago; dangerous if at his best.
Disappointing when odds-on for a maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good) on Tuesday; his third-place finishes at Musselburgh and York beforehand were solid displays though and he's a leading contender.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late G E Dudfield
Spotlight: 33-1, slowly away and green in the race when sixth of eight in a 7f maiden here three weeks ago; should prove a different proposition in time; stable also run Dream On Kent.
80-1 and ran to a modest level in a maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago; no appeal.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £50,000 yearling; dam, 5f AW winner (RPR 55), half-sister to useful 5f-7f winner Run Boy Run, out of a 5f winning half-sister to Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Smooch; this Sands Of Mali colt is in very good hands and rates an interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 18-1, last of five in maiden at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) on his debut 24 days ago; lots more is required from this son of Perfect Power.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cotai Glory gelding; hinted at promise with a debut sixth over C&D in May but only 11th in 6f York seller 22 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Invincible Army colt; closely related to winners Disillusion (Spanish/French Listed-placed 5.5f/6f; RPR 103) and Inca Dream (Slovakian/Czech 5f/6f) and also a half-brother to KSA 6f/7f 2yo winner Thenayaan; dam unraced; the market can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Unique Retreats, P Morrall & J Blackburn
Spotlight: 160,000euros 2yo; dam 6f-1m winner (RPR 101), half-sister to winners Partitia (6f) and Palmetto Bay (German 1m3f-1m4f), out of a 6f winning sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Showcasing; tongue tied; this son of Blue Point can't be discounted, especially if the market vibes are positive.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Never dangerous in maidens at Thirsk (5f) and Leicester (7f) this summer; significantly more is needed from this son of Lope Y Fernandez.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 12-1 and hooded, very slowly away when fifth of seven in maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good) on her debut in May; this daughter of Caturra should be all the better for the experience.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Starman filly who has progressed with racing, runner-up in 5f Sandown novice 22 days ago; she holds good form claims; fourth Beverley 6.30 Friday.
Came good in Hong Kong at sixth attempt, with emphatic C&D success last month, but combination of 10lb rise and outside draw asks a lot of him today.
Ex-Australian 3yo; made just a mildly encouraging Hong Kong debut when keeping-on seventh here (6f) last month but that run might have brought him on and this longer trip ought to suit; interesting.
Scored over 6f in January but has been below that form lately (blinkers added last time) and needs to up his game on this first attempt at 7f.

Without a win since 2024 and much better known as a sprinter but was persistently denied a clear run and looked unlucky not to finish much closer than eighth over C&D in May; Zac Purton keeps the ride; no surprise if he features.

Placed three consecutive times in this higher grade after Class 4 C&D win in March and had possible ground excuse when below form on latest outing but might remain a shade vulnerable off a mark this high.
1-4 when based in Australia, the win gained in a 1m maiden in October; absent since placed over 1m2f the following month and might find 7f too sharp on this Hong Kong debut.

OwnerName: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay
Spotlight: Won twice over 6f in early part of this season and is also suited by 7f but needs to get back in the groove after some disappointing efforts this year.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Alan Leung Wai Zen
Spotlight: Placed twice in this higher grade since Class 4 C&D win in February and can be excused last month's defeat here (hampered/denied clear run); may still have a bigger run in him; shortlisted.

OwnerName: 25/26 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: 2-8 when based in Australia; struggled when a big-priced outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut and remains best watched.

Won 1m maiden on sole 2yo start in Ireland; made low-key start to Hong Kong career over 6f last month but had a tough draw and encountered some traffic; ought to be seen in a better light over this longer trip.

2-9 when trained in Australia; endured a wide trip before keeping on for third in competitive 6f contest on Hong Kong debut in May and was denied a clear run when close sixth over that trip a fortnight ago; sure to be suited by this extra furlong; might be the answer.

OwnerName: Anthony Poon Tak Ching, Vivica Poon Ip Sze Man et al
Spotlight: Well beaten on first two Hong Kong starts (both 6f); needs huge boost from the new cheekpieces.

OwnerName: 24/25 Pierre Ng Pang Chi Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: 0-11 in Hong Kong but kept on from the rear to take second over 6f at Happy Valley last month and looks well worth another crack at 7f; a possible.
Struggled on first two Hong Kong starts (6f/7f); can't be recommended.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 1m winner early in career but has shown best form at 1m1f/1m2f, notably with wins in the 2024 Cambridgeshire and Listed races last year; bit to prove off current mark back down in trip; wears first-time headgear.

Similar type to Cerulean Bay in that he ran creditably in the Royal Hunt Cup (fourth) last time but is getting little help from the assessor, having proved largely consistent since his last win; solid contender all the same.
Reasonably consistent, including at Listed/Group level, until running poorly in the Royal Hunt Cup most recently; perhaps best watched as he needs to bounce back from a heavy defeat.

Useful but he is 0-12 since a Doncaster novice double in spring 2024 and not particularly solid on 2026 form; on a difficult mark returned to handicap level and isn't sure to uphold a consistent Sandown record.

Prevailed by a neck from Indalo at Newbury in May; unable to confirm the placings in the Royal Hunt Cup but wasn't disgraced; successful on this card in 2023 (7f handicap) and 2025 (this race); solid claims back here.

Threatening to win a good prize; unlucky second in last year's Cambridgeshire and finished third, best of the far-side runners, in the Royal Hunt Cup most recently; 1-1 at Sandown; big chance provided the cards drop right.
Ex-French 5yo who did well to finish eighth in the Royal Hunt Cup (third British attempt) most recently, having been slowly away; may build on that effort, especially if breaking cleanly.
Ran respectably at Epsom last time but still has a poor strike-rate in recent years and hasn't won on turf since 2023; the 8yo looks unlikely to prevail in another competitive field.

Low-mileage 4yo who ran well in the C&D event won by Beagle Bay most recently but needs to build on that effort and to prove conclusively that his current mark is surmountable; balance of form gives doubt.

Low-mileage 4yo; showed useful form last month, winning well at Newbury and fifth (again from the front) dropped back to 7f in Royal Ascot handicap; remains of interest, with further progress still possible.

Seemed disappointing last time but perhaps had an issue as a tongue-tie is now fitted; has plenty of respectable handicap form otherwise and was an unlucky third in the Thirsk Hunt Cup earlier in May; not written off.
Landed the Thirsk Hunt Cup, albeit having enjoyed a smoother trip than unlucky Sea Force; not particularly solid on his form since; ended up two places behind River King at Royal Ascot most recently.

Five-time winner at about 1m; creditable third, despite traffic issues, in major handicaps at Doncaster and Ascot (7f) this term; possibilities off same mark back up in distance, provided this race pans out better.

OwnerName: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners
Spotlight: 3yo who is capable of useful form, most recently a rallying sixth in Royal Ascot handicap (1m2f); however, he still hasn't added to his early 2yo success and isn't crying out for this drop back in trip.
Has gained both handicap wins in tight finishes at Class 3 level, including at Redcar latest start; may well come up short in this better grade, for all that he receives weight all round.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Took a huge step forward from moderate debut when running Carry The Flag to within a head 56 days ago over C&D (deadheating with the smart Sun Goddess in second); front three pulled well clear so rates smart form; winner has improved since so it could be difficult to turn the tables but he's a danger with further progression.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Smart colt; took an age to get up and beat Abletai over C&D in May but proved much sharper next time over 6f when second to Coventry winner at the Curragh; ran okay when beaten 7l into ninth after going off 5-2 favourite in the Norfolk 14 days ago; clearly the most likely winner dropped in class; 5f might be on sharp side but stiff track plays to his strengths.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Placed in three maidens, best effort last time 27 days ago on first go on good ground when beaten 1.25l by Cleodolinda at Navan over 5f; tough ask to break maiden here; place claims.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Space Blues colt, cost 82,000euros as a 2yo; closely related to 6f 2yo/1m Listed winner Thunder Beauty (RPR 109), half-brother to three winners inc Haku (prolific 1m4f AW; 101) and Quita (German 1m3f-1m6f); dam German 1m3f/1m4f winner; well-bred colt has tall order on debut but market check advised for shrewd connections that excel with 2yos; one of two for stable.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Kept on late to be beaten 8l into sixth on debut at Navan 27 days ago, Hanney Boy 6.25l ahead; hard to see her making an impact at this stage at Listed level.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell
Spotlight: Ran to a useful level when second and third in two AW maidens but floundered in soft ground at the Curragh 76 days ago when last of six; return to decent ground will help but moderate place claims at best.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Useful form in first three starts, showed good attitude to get off the mark at York when beating subsequent winner Divine Whisper by a short head; backed up quickly five days later in the Queen Mary and couldn't make an impact in 15th but didn't disgrace herself; not discounted back in calmer waters.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Only glimpses of promise in three starts before improving markedly for return to 5f over C&D ten days ago, putting up an impressive 2.25l victory over April Mist under this rider having raced alone on the far side; form yet to be tested but she holds claims if building on it.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Promising debut when a short head second to Velozee at Cork (5f, soft) in April; well below that form when seventh here ten days ago, well held by Brazilian Diva on that run; needs to bounce back to hold any sort of a chance.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability on first two starts when fourth and third (latter a head behind Hanney Boy) at Dundalk and Cork (soft) before making all over C&D in May, form working out very well and Big Negotiator was 1.25l behind in third; below that when fifth in Lingfield novice 35 days ago, fading after being up with a strong pace; should bounce back and can be in the frame.

Went close in Listed race at York (1m4f, good to firm) last August before good third in Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (14.5f, good to soft); underwhelming in fifth on both starts this season but she'll be a danger to all if rediscovering her best 3yo form; a tongue-tie is added.

Runner-up in the Group 2 Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) in June 2024; missed all of last season and not at the top of her game on either outing this term, but she took a step back in the right direction at York (1m6f, good) last time when not seeing out the trip; not ruled out.

Lightly raced 5yo who was a Listed winner and Group 3 runner-up for the Gosdens last season; returned to form on second start for this yard when Listed fourth at Chantilly (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, two places and nearly 2l behind Tiffany; each-way chance in first-time tongue-tie.

Listed runner-up at Gowran Park (1m4f, good) last July on fourth start; soundly beaten in Listed race at Cork (10.4f, yielding) last September and sold out of Donnacha O'Brien's yard for 360,000gns in December; lightly raced and she's one to watch in the betting in first-time cheekpieces on her stable and seasonal debut, but improvement is necessary up in grade.

Progressive 3yo last season and she improved again to win a Listed race at Goodwood (1m4f, good) in May on her reappearance; this is tougher but the runner-up has franked that latest form and she could have more to offer for a trainer who excel with similar types; she's one to consider.

German 1m4f Group 2 winner who returned from an absence to finish second in two German Group 1s last autumn; reappeared with close Listed second at Chantilly (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, a run she may improve for, and she's the one to beat this afternoon; a visor replaces cheekpieces.

3yo who was a Listed winner at Naas (10.3f, good) in May when upped in trip and went close in the Group 3 Jannah Rose Stakes there (10.3f, good) ten days ago, when 5l clear of the third; needs to prove her stamina for this new distance but she might be far away if seeing it out.

Won two handicaps in the spring (7f/1m, AW/good to firm) and was short-headed in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) four weeks ago under Jack Callan (who is again unable to claim his 3lb); she's a 3yo on the up and her dam won at up to 1m4f, but a further chunk of improvement is needed.

Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last October on her second run and a respectable third of six in Listed race at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance; stumbled and unseated soon after the start when 50-1 for the Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot last month; her dam won the Oaks for the yard and she's open to further improvement, but it's necessary in today's company.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third in a useful 7f novice here on debut last August (ran green); heavily backed and won easily on his seasonal return at Yarmouth (6f, soft) three weeks ago; looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to a Group 3 winner; justified market support when overcoming inexperience to win a 6f maiden at Lingfield (AW) in January; easy to back and ran poorly at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) in April, after which he was gelded; tongue tied today; dangerous if back to the debut form.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two Catterick runs this year, finishing third on debut (7f, good to firm) before winning over 6f (good to soft) last month; open to further progress and he should make another bold bid.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Huge prices and tailed off in two runs over further last month.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and little short-term promise in a 5f AW maiden last month.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Good pedigree but she has a run to a modest level in two 6f fillies' novice events this summer.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Down the field in two 6f novice events last month; minor handicaps beckon.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 35,000gns foal; dam Listed-placed 1m4f winner (RPR 101); the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this son of Saxon Warrior.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000euros yearling; closely related to 1m AW winner Handshake (RPR 70) and half-brother to three other winners, notably Habana (6.5f-1m German 1,000 Guineas; RPR 110); dam 5f and 6f (Italian Group 3) winner (RPR 102); this Sea The Stars newcomer must enter calculations, especially if the market speaks in his favour.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Palace Pier colt who was much improved from his debut when second of seven in C&D novice 18 days ago; he can do better still so needs considering.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 6-1, promising start with fourth of ten in novice at Thirsk (7f, good) 34 days ago, racing wide and fading only late on; that form has been franked so this son of Palace Pier rates a big player with better to come.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 16-1 and he ran green when last of five in novice at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on his debut two weeks ago; this Too Darn Hot colt is in very good hands and can build on it here.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; Space Traveller filly who is a half-sister to three winners, notably Cowardofthecounty (6f/7f Group 3; RPR 105); unraced dam is out of a half-sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Footstepsinthesand; a considered newcomer, although yard also has Rosberg in here.

Well-bred filly who cost 3,700,000gns as a yearling; ran well in the Nell Gwyn kept to 7f at Newmarket on seasonal debut; showed no further progress in 1m Listed event at York since but is the sort who should do better still; one of the main players on ratings.

Smartly bred filly, by Dubawi out of a Group/Grade 1 winner for her connections; scored with more in hand than the narrow margin suggests at Wolverhampton (8.5f; handicap debut) in latest AW start; interesting turf debutante, being the type to improve further.
Rather exposed filly whose peak figures may not be entirely reliable and who gained sole success in modest maiden at Bath when facing her easiest task; opposed.

Kingman filly who has shown consistent form in 1m2f events this season, while giving the impression this return to 1m may prove ideal; showed her 2yo promise over this trip, winning on debut and good second next time; solid chance, assuming she takes well to first-time headgear.

Unbeaten in two starts, both over 7f (likely to be as effective at 1m); comfortably defied a penalty in Haydock novice event (soft) on seasonal debut; out of a Listed/Group 3 winner for her owner-breeder and brings potential to this rise in grade; adds interest.

Fared about as well as expected when an outsider in the 1,000 Guineas last time; has otherwise shown clear promise, namely an AW success then good effort in the Fred Darling at Newbury; may show further progress back down in class.

Made a winning reappearance in AW contest; mixed results back on turf since, most recently coming up well short even allowing for difficult draw in Royal Ascot handicap (1m); limitations seem exposed.
Not disgraced in Royal Ascot handicap (1m) last time but has become a touch exposed since the second of her two juvenile wins and faces a stiff task in this field; trip-wise, may prove best over 7f.
Always going well when 10/1 and 1st of 10 runners, 0.60 lengths ahead ISIVIVANE over 1900m at GREYVILLE on 09 May 26. Smart Wsb 1900 win-draw 1.
Carried-jck-chokeVN when 9/4 and 8th of 10 runners, 4.50 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Live up to expectation?.

OwnerName: Messrs Cameron Braun, E A Braun Et Al
Spotlight: Rolled-brush-missed when 7/1 and 2nd of 12 runners, 0.05 lengths behind QUESTIONING over 1600m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Top Gr1 efforts-weight...

OwnerName: D Dasrath & Mrs C Dasrath, R P Macnab Et Al
Spotlight: Roll out-bump-short when 10/1 and 8th of 11 runners, 11.40 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Good filly - can show.
Gutsy-now the July! when 5/10 and 1st of 7 runners, 0.05 lengths ahead WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Smashing filly-big say.

OwnerName: Messrs E G Hart De Keating, M L Jean Hardy Et Al
Spotlight: Ran on too late when 25/2 and 3rd of 12 runners, 2.15 lengths behind QUESTIONING over 1600m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. 7th in 2025 - solid prep.
Led - tired late when 16/1 and 4th of 11 runners, 4.00 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Honest sort-nice weight.

OwnerName: Greg & Gina Bortz, T Gujadhur & R M Sewgoolam
Spotlight: Rolled in-easy win when 9/4 and 1st of 9 runners, 1.05 lengths ahead NATIVE RULER over 2400m at GREYVILLE on 13 June 26. Top finish (6th) in 2025.

OwnerName: Messrs J I Bloch & Jonathan Snaith & Nancy Hossack
Spotlight: Moved up-stayed on when 14/10 and 3rd of 11 runners, 3.00 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Pretender to the throne?.
Eased early-topfin. when 6/1 and 1st of 10 runners, 0.70 lengths ahead ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Getting better with time.
Too short-no threat when 9/2 and 7th of 13 runners, 4.55 lengths behind QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Ideal prep for G1 winner.

OwnerName: Messrs E C Abraham, Mayesh Chetty Et Al
Spotlight: Slow - ran on well when 7/1 and 2nd of 10 runners, 0.70 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Honest form-dark horse.
Shifted in 400m when 28/10 and 4th of 11 runners, 1.80 lengths behind ALADDIN'S LAMP over 1800m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 07 June 26. Never far off-tries track.
Rein drop100-almost when 16/1 and 2nd of 7 runners, 0.05 lengths behind WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Almost pulled it off last.

OwnerName: Lucky Vest 12 Cc, D D Maclean Et Al
Spotlight: Stayed on when 5/1 and 4th of 6 runners, 3.00 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1400m at SCOTTSVILLE on 17 May 26. Classy-can mow em down.
Fought - crop fine when 25/2 and 1st of 11 runners, 0.50 lengths ahead ALADDIN'S LAMP over 1800m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 07 June 26. Could make things pay.
No extra-crop fine when 3/1 and 2nd of 6 runners, 0.60 lengths behind FIRE ATTACK over 2000m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 28 April 26. Reserve 1.
Ran on-looked winnr when 5/4 and 2nd of 9 runners, 0.25 lengths behind JAZZ CAFE over 2400m at GREYVILLE on 20 June 26. Reserve 2.

OwnerName: Annua Racing SL, Ecurie Altima & Marc-Antoine Berghgracgt
Yanis Tarzout, Mme Victoria Anfuso Et Al

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Gained second turf win over C&D on penultimate, beating Nouvel Espoir by 0.5l off 4lb lower; disappointed off revised mark at Dundalk latest; return to this track a plus but must prove she can win off this mark.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time winner including over C&D; no joy yet this season but ran better than the bare result from miles out of the handicap at the Curragh last weekend; this more realistic and lurking on a dangerous mark.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came in a 2yo maiden at Bellewstown in 2024; ran well at Dundalk in March but below best in first-time cheekpieces at Navan 27 days ago; blinkers re-applied now (ran poorly in them before).

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy
Spotlight: 5f Dundalk winner last season has yet to make much of an impact in handicap company; beaten 10l on latest at Fairyhouse (6f, good); eased 3lb and the drop back to 5f is a positive so not totally dismissed.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time turf winner in 2024, both at 5f on good to firm and good to yielding ground; is now 14lb below his last success and not in bad form when last seen at Dundalk in March; worth checking in the market on return to turf.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill
Spotlight: Gained fourth win of career on latest at Down Royal (5f, soft); effective on quicker ground so conditions no concern and has gone well here before but a 6lb rise makes things tougher (never won off a mark this high).

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best two runs in maidens came over 7f, including when beaten 4l here last September; underwhelming run on handicap debut at Cork (6f, yielding) later that month and off since; likely best watched on seasonal return and first try over 5f.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A few placed efforts to his name, including in a Navan nursery (6f, yielding) last season off 9lb higher; bitterly disappointing in maidens at Down Royal (7f, good) and Cork (5f, good) the last twice; mark sliding but revival needed.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Cork in May off 2lb lower (5f, soft); struggled off this mark twice since, including when well behind Nouvel Espoir at Down Royal on his favoured easy ground latest; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner is 0-5 on turf and has failed to trouble the judge; below best in three runs this year at Dundalk and tongue tie now reached for.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time winner including five on turf; last won over 6f (good) here in May 2025 off 65 and is 5lb lower now; went close in big field over this C&D (good) on penultimate; below par over 6f at Fairyhouse 3 days ago but could rebound back here with rider claiming valuable 7lb.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time AW winner is yet to score on turf (0-6); not disgraced over C&D (good) on latest and eased 5lb for that 4.25l defeat but more needed to gain first win on the grass.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2023 Royal Ascot winner who has also tasted the high life in top hurdle races; his attentions were turned back to the Flat for four races (UAE and Saudi Arabia) this January-March and he wasn't in peak form, before 12l tenth at Royal Ascot (1m4f; restless in the stalls) continued that trend; falling in the weights but needs a revival.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 10lb higher than when winning this race at its usual home Haydock (good) last July in great style; that was his final race last season but he put up a bold show when third to Klassleader at York this May; non-runner at Royal Ascot where the good to firm ground was deemed unsuitable (third of 12 on it at York last May); big shout if today's ground is okay.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Much improved last year, including after his second to Plage De Havre in this race at Haydock; York sixth to Klassleader on reappearance but back to his best when staying-on third of 16 at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm) 18 days ago; needs respect.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Often front-runner; won well over C&D on good to firm last July; somewhat back down the weights and in class when winning another Class 3 in seven-runner race at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm; made all) last Saturday in good style; back up 5lb for this Class 2 but each-way claims.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made debut in June 2025 and has had just five races (beaten a neck on good to firm on second start); came from the back when second on handicap debut at Haydock (1m6f, good to soft) in September and again when eventually well on top at York (1m4f, good; Plage De Havre third) on reappearance this May; up 8lb but retains significant potential.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won 1m4f/2m handicaps on the AW early this year; beaten just over 7l in hot handicaps at Carlisle (1m3f) and Royal Ascot (1m6f) on last two outings, shaping much better than ninth of 16 suggests in the latter (big move to dispute lead early in straight) but his turf strike-rate is 3-37.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Reliable and progressive on a variety of ground and courses, although has never raced at Newmarket; third in this race last year; hit new heights at Epsom (1m4f) on last two outings and makes each-way appeal.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fit from AW and hurdling when good second to Fierce Fortitude at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in April, keeping on strongly; respectable fourth of 16 to a runaway winner at Epsom (1m2f, good to soft) four weeks ago and should be okay back at 1m4f, but this race looks a bit too hot; 5lb claimer is recruited.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sole win from 15 races came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; sinking in the weights and well handicapped judged on plenty of 2025 French form, with a step back in the right direction when fifth of 14 at Sandown (1m2f) three weeks ago on his third start for current yard; cheekpieces go on and first crack at 1m4f looks interesting; a case can be made.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who scored from Ammes at Redcar when upped to 1m2f in April; creditable third of ten in the Zetland Gold Cup there (again 1m2f on good to firm) in May off this mark; very useful dam stayed 1m6f, so this new trip for her son could be within reach; may have more to give.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Second on good to firm last June (much better form since); well held when upped to 1m4f for a hot handicap on final start last season; gelded subsequently and won over 1m3f at Southwell (AW) and Hamilton (good; by 5l in good style) in his two races this term; up 8lb and into a much tougher contest, back at 1m4f, but maybe he's putting it all together now.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won last March/April on first two starts for yard; took just one of 16 races since but that was penultimate start (Beverley 1m2f, good to firm) and last month's third of 16 off this mark in a Class 2 at York (1m2f) was as good as he's ever been; stays 1m4f; not the percentage call but not impossible.

Started handicap life on an unrealistic mark after winning his first two on Tapeta and struggled last year; found his level again in the visor lately, chasing home a subsequent winner before finding 1m4f beyond him last time; one to consider.

OwnerName: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Struggled since winning a Polytrack maiden for the Crisford team last August and has come more than a stone down the weights for his current yard this season; goes beyond an extended 1m for the first time and is hard to recommend.

Goes well here but hasn't won in two years and become expensive to follow, finishing a beaten-favourite in two runs back; soft ground may have been behind his latest effort but he was withdrawn twice in May on account of good to firm, which is a worry.

Stamina doubts on pedigree upped to this trip last time but they proved unfounded, as she made all round Brighton in first-time cheekpieces; that wasn't much of a race and she's 5lb higher but she remains relatively unexposed.
Getting on and it's been a while since she won on turf; arrives in fair form but she only holds each-way claims dropped another 3lb.

Half-sister to a useful sprinter who's finished well held in three runs spanning the best part of a year (7f-1m2f); starts handicap life off a basement mark but would want to see some support before considering her.
Longstanding maiden who's done most racing over further, latterly 1m6f; not a bad effort in first-time cheekpieces on Polytrack in May but she's had any number of chances.

Ex-Irish filly who's got it together since upped to this trip back on turf, winning easily at similarly undulating Chepstow last weekend; may yet have more to offer and is shortlisted off a 6lb higher mark.

OwnerName: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer
Spotlight: Said to have been unsuited by very soft ground last time; today's stiffer test will suit on pedigree (out of a 2m winner) and she's worth tracking in the market now handicapping at a lowly level.
Never looked like getting close to Beryl's Girl at Brighton on her first crack at this trip 12 days ago; she settled better that day and is 7lb better off but has work to do to turn things round.
Mohaather filly who took a big step forward to gain a first win (at the fifth attempt) in 7f Musselburgh seller 12 days ago; still not taken lightly now going into nursery company.

Bayside Boy gelding who has been brought along steadily in his three runs, a never-dangerous sixth of ten dropped to 5f in novice at Windsor last month; appeals as a likely improver stepping up markedly in trip for his nursery debut with cheekpieces added; interesting.
Wells Farhh Go filly; only modest form shown in her three starts to date, eighth of 11 in 7f Chester seller three weeks ago; needs this switch to handicap company to spark improvement.
Has cut little ice in a trio of outings, last of nine in 6f Redcar novice two weeks ago; lots more is required from this daughter of Mattmu on her nursery debut.
Mohaather filly; only poor form shown thus far, eighth of ten in 6f Leicester maiden last month; cheekpieces go on for her handicap debut.

Progressive colt whose record is 3-6 and who has never finished out of the first two; comfortably bagged AW Listed honours on seasonal debut then posted a highly notable effort in the Brigadier Gerard Stakes over C&D (good) even allowing that he received 7lb from the neck winner Ombudsman, whose subsequent Royal Ascot performance puts him in contention for being the world's current best racehorse; sire won this race in 2020; brings strong claims to this first Group 1 attempt.

Capable of very useful form but he is 0-12 since landing a major handicap at Newbury over two years ago and was unplaced in the Listed Wolferton Stakes at Royal Ascot last month; supplemented at a cost of £50,000, possibly to act as pacemaker for fellow Wathnan Racing runner Gethin.

Progressive colt whose record is 4-8 and who has never finished out of the first three; beaten over 3l by similar type Gethin at Newbury early last season but holds a fighting chance in the rematch, having roughly caught up with that rival in terms of ratings; creditable third to sibling Almaqam in the Tattersalls Gold Cup at the Curragh (first Group 1 attempt) most recently; particularly interesting returned to Sandown, being 2-2 over C&D, namely wins in a useful handicap last August (soft) and this year's Gordon Richards Stakes (good).

Record of 0-6 since debut success doesn't tell the whole story as he's shown progressive form behind Ballydoyle colts, including the subsequent Derby winner, in last four appearances; did well to finish fourth in the French Derby (10.5f, good) most recently, having met traffic issues and come from a lot further back than the first three; provided the cards drop more kindly, has possibilities in this intriguing rematch with Constitution River and Hawk Mountain.

OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Ballydoyle colt who went close in a strong maiden on debut and is 4-4 since, most recently beating stablemate Hawk Mountain by just under 1l in the Prix du Jockey Club at Chantilly (10.5f); impressive at Chester the time before and that Listed form has worked out well; top on the figures, still improving to boot and represents a trainer who has landed this prize nine times, including with 3yos (among them a French Derby winner) in four of the last five years; duly much respected.

OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Capable of useful form but is well down the pecking order at Ballydoyle and 0-6 since debut win; never-dangerous 11th of 30, fitted with cheekpieces, in Royal Ascot handicap (1m) most recently; headgear retained; clear bottom of this pack on ratings and is possibly in here as pacemaker for his two stablemates.

OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Record of 4-6 includes three Group wins, notably the Futurity at Doncaster last autumn; has taken well to headgear (cheekpieces then blinkers) in two races at Chantilly this season, good second to stablemate Constitution River in the French Derby (10.5f, good) latest; no certainty to reverse placings with that colt, who is still improving, but is building up a solid record and again has place possibilities.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Mehmas colt ran a promising race when a close third on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good) before justifying skinny odds at Fairyhouse (7f, good), keeping on well (form boosted by the third since); dam a 1m1f winner as a 2yo so this trip should be within reach; obvious chance for top yard.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Excellent effort when runner up on debut at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld); failed to figure in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot latest (6f, gd-fm); now steps up in trip (half-brother won on dirt over 1m); too soon to write off, preferred by McMonagle and could go well.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Rectory Road Holdings Limited & Mrs A M O'Brien
Spotlight: Stable second string on debut at Gowran (7f, soft) and well beaten into third behind fancied stablemate; did shape like further would suit so the step up to 1m is a plus and his dam was a winner at this trip on good ground but major improvement required.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Modest debut run at Dundalk in April; runner up when 16-1 in class 2 novice at York (6f, good) but subsequently disqualified (misuse of whip); yard in form and the form of that York race is solid but whether he'll stay 1m is the question (mixed messages in pedigree).

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly; 58,000gns yearling, E100,000 2yo; fourth foal; half-sister to Qatari 5f-6.5f winner Temple Of Hera; dam 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 76), half-sister to winners Electric Waves (5f 2yo Group 3) and Appealing (useful 6f-1m); speedily bred so stamina a query and thrown in at the deep end on debut; market can guide.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well before fading into fifth of 10 on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good); pedigree suggests this trip might stretch here and major improvement needed upped in class.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Wooded filly; E41,000 yearling; half-sister to French 6f-7.5f winner Viva La Skids (inc 2yo; RPR 103); dam unplaced 6f-1m2f in France, out of French 1m1f winning half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Desert Blanc; yard's 2yos always respected but looks yard's second string on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won over 7.5f on debut at Roscommon; not disgraced in 6f Group 3 at this venue in May but disappointed stepped back up to 7f at Fairyhouse on latest and McDonogh now jumps ship; however, bred to appreciate this longer trip and a repeat of her Group 3 run here would give her a place shout.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row in May (7f, good to firm) and he returned to winning ways when back down in trip at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) eight days ago; this progressive 4yo could take a 4lb rise in his stride.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Placed on first two starts of the season (6f, good) and another big performance when 3l fourth of 20 at York (6f, good) last time, which was his third quick run in succession in May; can go well for bang-in-form yard.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Formerly trained in Hong Kong; won at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May on third stable start and reportedly unsuited by the good to soft ground when down the field at Newbury (6f) since; could bounce back.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Thirsk (6f, good) in April on second stable start and second of 20 at York (6f, good) in May, before respectable fifth of 14 at Thirsk (6f, good); uncharacteristically below par at York three weeks ago but not written off in view of his previous exploits.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Two turf wins last summer and three AW wins this year, the latest at Wolverhampton in April; ran well when third of five at Salisbury next time but two lesser runs have followed and he needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: This C&D brought out the best in him last season with two close seconds in August (both on good to firm); returned to form with close fourth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month on his second start of the season and he could play a leading role back here.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: 7yo who often ran well in the US, albeit winning just once (6.5f, firm) back in 2023; a market check is advised on his British debut but it's possible he's best watched.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: His last three wins have come off a similar mark to today's over 6f on the Newmarket courses and he returned to form with a close third here (7f, good) a fortnight ago; every chance he'll be in the mix.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Won at Hamilton (6f, good) last month on second start back from a break and the neck runner-up went one better there next time; he's used to plying to his trade in a lower grade but is not discounted up just 2lb.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: In good form since returning from a break, winning at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May before close third at Salisbury (6f, soft); there's still mileage in his mark on his old form and he could be in the shake-up.

Smart performer with a decent strike-rate, who comes into this off a clear career best when landing a Group 2 at York (6f, good ground) when the headgear was back on; going unlikely to be very soft judged by the weather forecast plus this race is later in the day, so only the 4lb and more he has to give away the concern; Mickael Barzalona rides for first time.

Running well this year and was only half a length behind Stolen Kiss (and ahead of two of this field) when last seen two months ago (6f, good to soft), with that rivals going on to finish a close fourth at Royal Ascot recently in a Group 1; should give a solid account and stays 7f, but wins so far have been in a maiden, handicap and a conditions race.

Not had much racing and been progressing nicely, with his most recent start a win over 6f on heavy ground at Chantilly in a conditions event; this is quite a jump in class, however, and improvement is required.
Nicely bred, being out of Wizz Kid, who notably won the Prix de l'Abbaye in 2012, but hasn't kicked on since her debut success and this looks a very hard task to add another win to her tally.

Capable of smart form, and wasn't beaten too far by the in-form Stolen Kiss a couple of times earlier in the year, although behind some of these, before proving 4.5l too good for Zzodiac a month ago over 6f on soft ground; going to need plenty more to win at this level and work to do with a couple of these on earlier meets in 2026.

Another in this field who had Stolen Kiss ahead of her twice this season over 6f, finishing behind Mise En Boite last time but ahead of Charibelle, with the latter winning since; overall record suggests he'll struggle toad to her tally at this level.

Supplemented for this; had some good form in 2025 and been running okay this campaign until blitzed, like everyone was, in the Palace Of Holyroodhouse last month behind Bacio; finished ahead of Kailani in a Chantilly Group 3 earlier in the year and should be in the mix for a place at least on his first run over 6f this season and blinkers added; first time Mickael Barzalona hasn't been aboard.

Knows how to win and mostly run creditably in 2026 before recording a clear career best 34 days ago in a Listed event at Chantilly over 6f on good ground; seems to handle most going, so is one of the more likelier types to land the prize considering she comes out top on adjusted figures.

Ffos Las (6f, good) win on debut; close fifth in a 7f AW handicap in January represents her best form but two heavy defeats have followed; has her first run in a 0-70 and with a tongue-tie added; not solid.
